user_setup:
  - service: supabase
    why: "Database backend for flag data persistence"
    env_vars:
      - name: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
        source: "Supabase Dashboard -> Settings -> API -> Project URL"
      - name: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
        source: "Supabase Dashboard -> Settings -> API -> Project API keys -> anon public"
    dashboard_config:
      - task: "Create a Supabase project (free tier)"
        location: "https://supabase.com/dashboard -> New Project"

    - "flags table exists in Supabase with correct schema"
    - "TypeScript types for Flag and ImportResult are defined"
    - "Vitest runs and reports 0 tests (infrastructure ready)"

    - path: "supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql"

      contains: "create table"
    - path: "vitest.config.ts"
      provides: "Test framework configuration"
      contains: "vitest"
    - path: "public/templates/sample-flags.csv"
      provides: "Downloadable sample template (D-06)"

      pattern: "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL"
    - from: "src/lib/types/flag.ts"
      to: "supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql"
      via: "schema alignment"
      pattern: "row_label.*text"
---

<objective>
Scaffold the entire Next.js 16 project with all dependencies, configure Supabase client utilities, define TypeScript types and database schema, install shadcn/ui components, set up Vitest test infrastructure, and create test fixtures.

Purpose: Establish the complete project foundation so that all subsequent plans (parsing, import UI, directory) can build on a working project with types, database, and test infrastructure.
Output: A running Next.js project with Supabase connected, types defined, DB schema pushed, shadcn/ui ready, Vitest configured, and sample fixtures created.
</objective>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Scaffold Next.js project and install all dependencies</name>
  <files>
    package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.ts, src/app/layout.tsx, src/app/page.tsx,
    src/app/globals.css, components.json,
    src/components/ui/button.tsx, src/components/ui/dialog.tsx, src/components/ui/table.tsx,
    src/components/ui/card.tsx, src/components/ui/alert.tsx, src/components/ui/badge.tsx,
    src/components/ui/separator.tsx, src/components/ui/tabs.tsx, .env.local.example
  </files>
  <read_first>
    .planning/phases/01-data-foundation/01-RESEARCH.md (installation commands, SheetJS CDN note)
    .planning/phases/01-data-foundation/01-UI-SPEC.md (shadcn components list, design system)
    CLAUDE.md (technology stack, version compatibility)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --yes --ts --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --turbopack --import-alias "@/*"` from the project root to scaffold Next.js 16 with TypeScript, Tailwind v4, ESLint, App Router, src directory, and Turbopack.

    2. Install core dependencies:
       ```
       npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr
       npm install react-dropzone react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers zod
       npm install lucide-react
       npm install https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/xlsx-0.20.3.tgz
       ```
       CRITICAL: SheetJS MUST be installed from CDN tarball, NOT from npm registry. The npm `xlsx` v0.18.5 is stale and has vulnerabilities.

    3. Install dev dependencies:
       ```
       npm install -D prettier prettier-plugin-tailwindcss vitest @vitejs/plugin-react
       ```

    4. Initialize shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn@latest init --yes`
       Then add components: `npx shadcn@latest add button dialog table card alert badge separator tabs`

    5. Create `.env.local.example` with these exact contents:
       ```
       # Supabase (get from Supabase Dashboard -> Settings -> API)
       NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=your-project-url
       NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=your-anon-key
       ```

    6. Update `src/app/page.tsx` to redirect to `/admin`:
       ```typescript
       import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
       export default function Home() {
         redirect("/admin");
       }
       ```

    7. Update `src/app/layout.tsx` to load Inter font via `next/font/google` per UI-SPEC (shadcn default font). Keep the Tailwind CSS import. Set metadata title to "Gravestone Finder - Flag Data Import".

    NOTE: If `create-next-app` prompts interactively despite `--yes`, use default answers for all prompts.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd "C:/Users/2fire/All Coding/gravestone finder" && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5</automated>
  </verify>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Create types, Supabase clients, database migration, and sample template</name>
  <files>
    src/lib/types/flag.ts, src/lib/types/import-result.ts,
    src/lib/supabase/client.ts, src/lib/supabase/server.ts,
    supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql,
    public/templates/sample-flags.csv
  </files>
  <read_first>
    .planning/phases/01-data-foundation/01-RESEARCH.md (schema design, Supabase client pattern, row normalization)
    .planning/phases/01-data-foundation/01-CONTEXT.md (D-04 no auth, D-06 sample template, D-08 three columns, D-09 row normalization, D-12 Supabase storage)
    package.json (verify @supabase/ssr is installed)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    1. Create `src/lib/types/flag.ts` with these exact exports:
       ```typescript
       export interface Flag {
         id: number;
         name: string;
         row_label: string;
         position: number;
         created_at: string;
       }

       export type FlagInsert = Omit<Flag, "id" | "created_at">;
       ```

    2. Create `src/lib/types/import-result.ts` with these exact exports:
       ```typescript
       import type { FlagInsert } from "./flag";

       export interface ImportError {
         row: number;
         issues: string[];
       }

       export interface ImportResult {
         valid: FlagInsert[];
         errors: ImportError[];
         warnings: string[];
       }
       ```

    3. Create `src/lib/supabase/client.ts` (browser client, per D-04 no auth):
       ```typescript
       import { createBrowserClient } from "@supabase/ssr";

       export function createClient() {
         return createBrowserClient(
           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!
         );
       }
       ```

    4. Create `src/lib/supabase/server.ts` (server client):
       ```typescript
       import { createServerClient } from "@supabase/ssr";
       import { cookies } from "next/headers";

       export async function createClient() {
         const cookieStore = await cookies();
         return createServerClient(
           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
           process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!,
           {
             cookies: {
               getAll() {
                 return cookieStore.getAll();
               },
               setAll(cookiesToSet) {
                 try {
                   cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
                     cookieStore.set(name, value, options)
                   );
                 } catch {
                   // Safe to ignore in Server Components
                 }
               },
             },
           }
         );
       }
       ```

    5. Create `supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql` with this exact SQL:
       ```sql
       -- flags table: core data from CSV import
       create table public.flags (
         id bigint generated always as identity primary key,
         name text not null,
         row_label text not null,
         position integer not null,
         created_at timestamptz default now(),

         -- Composite unique constraint for merge/upsert (D-13) and duplicate detection (IMP-02)
         unique(row_label, position)
       );

       -- Full-text search support (for Phase 2, created now to avoid migration later)
       alter table public.flags
       add column fts tsvector generated always as (
         to_tsvector('english', name)
       ) stored;

       -- GIN index for fast full-text search
       create index flags_fts_idx on public.flags using gin (fts);

       -- Index for directory queries (ORG-01: alphabetical and by-row)
       create index flags_name_idx on public.flags (name);
       create index flags_row_position_idx on public.flags (row_label, position);

       -- RLS: Enable but allow all access (D-04: no auth needed)
       alter table public.flags enable row level security;

       create policy "Allow public read" on public.flags
         for select using (true);

       create policy "Allow public insert" on public.flags
         for insert with check (true);

       create policy "Allow public delete" on public.flags
         for delete using (true);

       create policy "Allow public update" on public.flags
         for update using (true);
       ```

    6. Create `public/templates/sample-flags.csv` (D-06) with this exact content:
       ```
       Name,Row,Position
       John Smith,A,1
       Jane Doe,A,2
       Robert Johnson,B,1
       Mary Williams,B,2
       James Brown,C,1
       ```

    7. Create directory structure: `mkdir -p supabase/migrations src/lib/types src/lib/supabase public/templates`
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd "C:/Users/2fire/All Coding/gravestone finder" && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -10</automated>
  </verify>

    - src/lib/supabase/server.ts contains `createServerClient`
    - src/lib/supabase/server.ts contains `cookies`
    - supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql contains `create table public.flags`
    - supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql contains `unique(row_label, position)`
    - supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql contains `to_tsvector`
    - supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql contains `enable row level security`
    - supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql contains `Allow public read`
    - public/templates/sample-flags.csv contains `Name,Row,Position`
    - `npx tsc --noEmit` passes without errors
  </acceptance_criteria>
  <done>TypeScript types defined and exported, Supabase browser and server clients created, database migration SQL written with flags table (including FTS column, indexes, and permissive RLS), and sample CSV template placed in public directory.</done>

<task type="checkpoint:human-action" gate="blocking">
  <name>Task 3: Supabase project setup and schema push</name>

  <action>
    User must complete the following steps to connect the application to Supabase and create the database schema. Claude cannot do this because it requires Supabase account credentials and dashboard access.

    1. Create a Supabase project at https://supabase.com/dashboard if one does not exist.
    2. Copy `.env.local.example` to `.env.local` and fill in credentials from the Supabase dashboard.
    3. Push the database schema using Supabase CLI (`supabase db push`) or by running the SQL in `supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql` in the Supabase Dashboard SQL Editor.
    4. Verify the flags table exists in Supabase Dashboard Table Editor.
  </action>

  <files>

  <action>
    1. Create `vitest.config.ts` at project root:
       ```typescript
       import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config";
       import react from "@vitejs/plugin-react";
       import path from "path";

       export default defineConfig({
         plugins: [react()],
         test: {
           environment: "jsdom",
           include: ["src/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/*.test.tsx"],
           globals: true,
         },
         resolve: {
           alias: {
             "@": path.resolve(__dirname, "./src"),
           },
         },
       });
       ```

    2. Add test script to `package.json` scripts section:
       ```json
       "test": "vitest run",
       "test:watch": "vitest"
       ```

    3. Create test fixture `src/__fixtures__/sample-valid.csv`:
       ```
       Name,Row,Position
       John Smith,A,1
       Jane Doe,A,2
       Robert Johnson,B,1
       Mary Williams,B,2
       James Brown,C,1
       Patricia Davis,C,2
       Michael Wilson,1,3
       Linda Martinez,2,4
       ```
       Note: Rows "1" and "2" are numeric -- tests for D-09 row normalization.

    4. Create test fixture `src/__fixtures__/sample-with-errors.csv`:
       ```
       Name,Row,Position
       John Smith,A,1
       ,A,2
       Jane Doe,,3
       Robert Johnson,B,
       Mary Williams,B,1
       Valid Person,C,1
       Another Valid,C,2
       ```
       Note: Row 2 has no name, row 3 has no row, row 4 has no position. Row 5 duplicates (B,1) with row 4 only if row 4 were valid.

    5. Create directory: `mkdir -p src/__fixtures__`
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd "C:/Users/2fire/All Coding/gravestone finder" && npx vitest run --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -5</automated>

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Browser -> Supabase | Client-side Supabase SDK connects with anon key; all operations go through RLS |
| User file -> Browser | Untrusted spreadsheet files loaded into browser memory |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-01-01 | Tampering | supabase/migrations/00001_create_flags.sql | mitigate | RLS enabled with explicit public policies. When auth is added later, policies must be tightened. For now, D-04 explicitly requires no auth -- accept public write access. |
| T-01-02 | Information Disclosure | .env.local | mitigate | .env.local.example provided without real credentials. .env.local must be in .gitignore (create-next-app includes it by default). Publishable key is safe to expose (it's the anon key). |
| T-01-03 | Denial of Service | Supabase free tier | accept | Free tier has rate limits. For a single-event app with limited users, this is acceptable. Monitor during event. |
</threat_model>

</verification>
